name: Smurfit Westrock Plc
executive_summary: Smurfit Westrock is a global paper-based packaging leader formed by the July 2024 combination of Smurfit Kappa and WestRock, operating across North America, Europe/MEA/APAC and Latin America with vertically integrated mills and converting assets. The quantitative screen flags a buy on cash yield, dividend income, composite value and financial health (5/22 models; 66% composite), supported by a ~5.8% FCF yield, 4.2% dividend yield and P/B of 1.3x. The valuation hook is a scaled, asset-backed franchise trading below intrinsic value on cash metrics, while headline P/E (61.8x) and ROE (2.1%) are distorted by merger integration costs, restructuring and cyclical softness. The central debate is whether synergy delivery, mill closures and a stated medium-term cash-return plan can offset near-term margin pressure, ~$14bn gross debt and ongoing restructuring charges evident in 2025–2026 interim results.
investment_thesis:
For a value investor, SW offers exposure to a top-tier corrugated and consumer packaging platform at a modest book multiple, with tangible cash return characteristics that align with the screen’s buy signal. The screen passes four factor families—cheapness, dividend, GARP and risk—via FCF Yield, Neff PEGY (0.19), High Dividend Yield, Composite Value and Financial Health, with high data quality (19/20 metrics). Business quality is underpinned by scale (57 paper mills, 450 converting plants across 40 countries, per the FY2025 10-K body), geographic diversification and exposure to fibre-based packaging substitution trends.

The value case rests on three pillars visible in filings and screen data: (1) cash generation at scale—Yahoo reports 2025 operating cash flow of $3.39bn and FCF of $1.20bn (used as fallback; see Financial Review); (2) shareholder yield—$900m dividends paid in 2025 (Yahoo) and $237m in Q1 2026 alone (Q1 2026 10-Q); and (3) balance-sheet asset backing at ~1.3x P/B with total assets of ~$45bn. Integration remains the swing factor: segment Adjusted EBITDA in Q1 2026 was $1.13bn versus $1.29bn in Q1 2025 (Q1 2026 10-Q), with North America weaker but Europe/MEA/APAC improving. If closure programmes and synergies convert to sustained margin recovery, the screen’s cheapness signal should prove durable; if not, the buy case becomes a dividend-and-asset floor rather than an earnings rerating.
financial_review:
**Primary source note:** Annual 10-K body extracts (FY2024 filed March 2025; FY2025 filed February 2026) are truncated before Item 7 MD&A and Item 8 financial statements. Annual income, cash-flow and balance-sheet figures below are therefore taken from `financials_annual.json` (Yahoo) with explicit fallback. Interim figures are from SEC 10-Q body extracts where available.

**Annual results (post-merger baseline)**

| Metric | FY2025 | FY2024 | FY2023 (Smurfit Kappa standalone) |
|--------|--------|--------|-----------------------------------|
| Revenue | $31.18bn | $21.11bn | $12.09bn |
| Operating income | $2.22bn | $1.46bn | $1.48bn |
| Net income | $699m | $319m | $825m |
| EBITDA (reported) | $4.35bn | $2.55bn | $1.89bn |
| Free cash flow | $1.20bn | $17m | $630m |
| Total debt | $13.77bn | $13.60bn | $3.75bn |
| Net debt | $12.88bn | $12.74bn | $2.75bn |
| Total assets | $45.16bn | $43.76bn | $14.05bn |
| Cash | $892m | $855m | $1.00bn |
| Dividends paid | $900m | $650m | $391m |

*(Source: Yahoo fallback for all annual figures above.)*

FY2025 revenue reflects a full year of the combined entity versus a part-year in FY2024 (Combination completed 5 July 2024, per FY2025 10-K body). Reported net income improved to $699m but remains well below pre-merger Smurfit standalone earnings, weighed down by $505m unusual items including $246m write-offs and $259m restructuring/M&A charges (Yahoo). FCF recovered sharply to $1.20bn in 2025 from near-zero in 2024, when merger-related outflows and working-capital absorption constrained conversion.

**Interim trends (filings)**

*Q1 2026 (10-Q, filed 1 May 2026):*
- Net sales: $7,712m vs $7,656m (+1% YoY)
- Operating profit: $253m vs $553m (−54%)
- Net income attributable to shareholders: $65m vs $384m
- Diluted EPS: $0.12 vs $0.73
- Impairment and restructuring: $54m vs $15m
- Segment Adjusted EBITDA: $1,127m vs $1,289m (North America $597m vs $785m; Europe/MEA/APAC $421m vs $389m; LATAM $109m vs $115m)
- Operating cash flow: $204m vs $235m; capex: $624m vs $477m
- Cash: $674m; total debt ~$14.3bn (current $980m + non-current $13,275m)
- Dividends paid: $237m

*Nine months ended 30 September 2025 (10-Q, filed 7 November 2025):*
- Net sales: $23,599m vs $13,570m (prior year not fully comparable pre-merger)
- Operating profit: $1,330m vs $606m
- Net income: $602m vs $173m
- Impairment and restructuring (9M): $360m vs $21m
- Q3 2025 alone: net sales $8,003m; operating profit $526m vs $55m; net income $246m vs loss of $150m

*Q2 2025 (10-Q, filed 7 August 2025):*
- Q2 net sales $7,940m; operating profit $251m after $280m impairment/restructuring in the quarter
- H1 2025 net sales $15,596m; net income $356m

**Balance sheet and pensions (Q1 2026 10-Q, 31 March 2026):**
- Total assets: $45.17bn; goodwill $7.19bn
- Pension and post-retirement liabilities (non-current): $686m; prepaid pension asset $642m
- No going-concern qualification identified in available filing extracts

**Gaps:** FY2025 and FY2024 10-K bodies lack audited financial tables; eight 8-K filings in the index have no body extracts; Q3 2025 body is truncated mid-discussion of April 2025 announced mill closures.
risks_and_flags:
**Integration and restructuring.** Filings repeatedly cite synergy delivery, announced closures and “additional economic downtime” as material uncertainties. Nine-month 2025 impairment and restructuring costs reached $360m (Q3 2025 10-Q). Q1 2026 operating profit fell sharply despite flat revenue, indicating margin compression from cost inflation, downtime and closure-related charges.

**Leverage and covenants.** Risk-factor language in interim filings flags the Company’s “significant levels of indebtedness,” potential credit-rating impact from the Combination, and dependency on “sufficient cash to distribute dividends…in line with current expectations.” Gross debt of ~$14.3bn at March 2026 against cash of $674m leaves limited liquidity buffer; Q1 2026 operating cash flow of $204m did not cover $624m capex and $237m dividends.

**Legacy litigation and tax.** Asbestos-related litigation: ~770 lawsuits outstanding at 31 March 2026; costs “not been significant” to date but filings warn costs could rise materially if the litigation profile changes (Q1 2026 10-Q). Brazil tax liability: WestRock is contesting CARF assessments on goodwill amortisation for tax years 2003–2012, with penalties assessed including fraud claims in one proceeding (Q1 2026 10-Q). Environmental contingencies are referenced in risk factors.

**Cyclical and macro exposure.** Forward-looking statements cite inflation in energy, raw materials and labour; customer inventory rebalancing; geo-economic fragmentation; and demand volatility—standard for containerboard but acute during integration.

**Goodwill and impairment.** $7.2bn goodwill on the balance sheet (Q1 2026) against a backdrop of $360m YTD restructuring in 2025 raises impairment risk if North American recovery stalls.

**Governance/listing.** NYSE-only listing after London delisting (June 2026 news) simplifies the capital structure but reduces European investor access—a secondary consideration rather than a balance-sheet risk.
news_highlights:
Coverage is moderate for SW specifically; the news manifest contains substantial ticker noise (e.g. Sodexo ENXTPA:SW, Santhera SANN.SW, geographic “SW London” articles). Material SW-specific items include:

- **“Smurfit Westrock maps $14B cash plan, $5B in dividends by 2030”** (Stock Titan, 11 February 2026) — management’s medium-term capital allocation framework.
- **“Smurfit Westrock delists from London Stock Exchange”** / **“Ends UK Listing, Moves to NYSE-Only Trading Structure”** (Investing.com / Moomoo, 22 June 2026) and related Simply Wall St pieces on how the sole NYSE listing changes the investment story (23–24 June 2026).
- **“Smurfit Westrock Falls Short of Earnings & Revenue Estimates in Q1”** (Yahoo Finance, 30 April 2025) and **“Q1 Earnings Call Highlights”** (Yahoo Finance, 2 May 2025) — near-term miss against consensus.
- **“Smurfit Westrock Bets On A 2030 Packaging Turnaround”** (Finimize, 2 June 2025) — strategic repositioning narrative.
- **“Smurfit Westrock: Demand Inflection And Pricing Power Support Upside Despite Near-Term Pressure”** (Seeking Alpha, 2 May 2025).
- **Coca-Cola China paper packaging partnership** referenced in Yahoo Finance (15 July 2026) — commercial validation of sustainable packaging offering.
- **Barclays Overweight, PT cut to $52 from $54** (Yahoo Finance, 30 May 2025).
- **“What You Need To Know Ahead of Smurfit Westrock’s Earnings Release”** (Yahoo Finance, 8 July 2026) — Q2 FY2026 expectations for double-digit earnings growth.

Overall, news flow confirms an integration-and-restructuring story with a long-dated cash-return commitment, offset by Q1 2025 earnings disappointment and share-price underperformance versus packaging peers.
research_rationale: Deep research supports the screen’s buy signal on FCF yield, dividend and asset backing at a global packaging leader, but near-term earnings volatility, restructuring charges and leverage temper conviction pending clearer post-integration margin recovery.
